title: Choose a shard key for even write distribution
description: Pick a high-cardinality, non-monotonic shard key so writes and data spread evenly instead of hotspotting one shard.
body: Use when enabling sharding on a collection or diagnosing an overloaded shard. Do not shard on a monotonically increasing field like a timestamp or ObjectId alone; all new writes land in the highest chunk and hammer a single shard. Actions: choose a high-cardinality key the queries actually filter on, use a hashed shard key or a compound key to break monotonic ordering, and verify chunk distribution with sh.status(). Validation signals include chunks spread across shards and write throughput scaling with shard count. Risk boundary: the shard key is immutable on older versions and expensive to reshard, so model it deliberately and get review before sharding a large collection.
